API Reference

Arbiter HTTP API`GET /v1/a2a/agents/:id/agent-card.json``POST /v1/a2a/agents/:id``GET /.well-known/agent-card.json``GET /v1/admin/audit``POST /v1/admin/tenants``GET /v1/admin/tenants/:id``GET /v1/admin/tenants``PATCH /v1/admin/tenants/:id``POST /v1/agents/:id/chat``POST /v1/agents``DELETE /v1/agents/:id``GET /v1/agents/:id``GET /v1/agents``PATCH /v1/agents/:id``POST /v1/conversations/:id/artifacts``DELETE /v1/conversations/:id/artifacts/:aid``GET /v1/conversations/:id/artifacts/:aid``GET /v1/conversations/:id/artifacts``GET /v1/conversations/:id/artifacts/:aid/raw``DELETE /v1/artifacts/:aid``GET /v1/artifacts/:aid``GET /v1/artifacts``GET /v1/artifacts/:aid/raw``POST /v1/conversations``DELETE /v1/conversations/:id``GET /v1/conversations/:id``GET /v1/conversations``GET /v1/conversations/:id/messages``POST /v1/conversations/:id/messages``PATCH /v1/conversations/:id``POST /v1/events``GET /v1/health``POST /v1/lessons``DELETE /v1/lessons/:id``GET /v1/lessons/:id``GET /v1/lessons``PATCH /v1/lessons/:id``POST /v1/memory/entries``DELETE /v1/memory/entries/:id``GET /v1/memory/entries/:id``POST /v1/memory/entries/:id/invalidate``GET /v1/memory/entries``PATCH /v1/memory/entries/:id``GET /v1/memory/:agent_id``GET /v1/memory/graph``GET /v1/memory``POST /v1/memory/relations``DELETE /v1/memory/relations/:id``GET /v1/memory/relations``GET /v1/metrics``GET /v1/models``GET /v1/notifications/stream``POST /v1/orchestrate``POST /v1/requests/:id/cancel``GET /v1/requests/:id/events``GET /v1/requests/:id``GET /v1/requests``GET /v1/runs/:id``GET /v1/runs``POST /v1/schedules``DELETE /v1/schedules/:id``GET /v1/schedules/:id``GET /v1/schedules``PATCH /v1/schedules/:id``GET /v1/schedules/:id/runs``POST /v1/todos``DELETE /v1/todos/:id``GET /v1/todos/:id``GET /v1/todos``PATCH /v1/todos/:id`

Philosophy

Philosophy
CLI / Arbiter CLI

Arbiter CLI

Arbiter is a single binary with three operating modes — interactive, one-shot, and server — plus a handful of admin commands. Which mode you pick depends on whether you want a terminal session, a scripted invocation, or a long-running HTTP service.

You want…Run
Interactive multi-pane TUIarbiter
One agent reply, piped output, no TTYarbiter --send <agent> <message>
HTTP+SSE orchestration server for other clientsarbiter --api [--port N] [--bind ADDR]
Initialize ~/.arbiter/ with example agentsarbiter --init
Manage --api tenant identitiesarbiter --add-tenant, --list-tenants, --disable-tenant, --enable-tenant

The interactive mode is documented separately under docs/tui. Everything else is here.

Full flag reference

arbiter                                    Interactive REPL (the TUI)
arbiter --init                             Create ~/.arbiter/ + example agents
arbiter --send <agent> <message>           One-shot — print agent reply to stdout
arbiter --api [--port N] [--bind ADDR]     HTTP+SSE orchestration server
              [--verbose]
arbiter --add-tenant <name>                Provision a tenant + API key
arbiter --list-tenants                     Print active and disabled tenants
arbiter --disable-tenant <id|name>         Revoke a tenant's access
arbiter --enable-tenant <id|name>          Restore access
arbiter --help                             Print built-in help

Pages

  • Getting started — pick a path:
  • Local install — install + first run on your own machine.
  • --init — what gets created, the ~/.arbiter/ layout.
  • --send — the one-shot mode for scripts, pipes, and CI hooks.
  • --api — the HTTP server mode: flags, behaviour, links to the endpoint docs.
  • Tenant admin — managing --api bearer tokens.
  • A2A agents — outbound remote-agent registry + the /a2a slash command.
  • Environment — every env var arbiter reads, what overrides what.

Choosing a mode

Interactive. The default. Multi-pane TUI, persistent session per cwd, all slash commands available. Built for humans typing into a terminal. Reach for it when exploring, iterating with an agent, or running long sessions.

One-shot (--send). No TTY, no panes, no streaming. Sends one message to one agent, prints the reply on stdout, exits non-zero on error. Built for scripts and pipelines:

echo "$DIFF" | arbiter --send reviewer "$(cat)"
arbiter --send research "summarise the top 3 issues" > /tmp/summary.md

The agent runs the full orchestration loop internally (tool calls, sub-agent delegation) — you just don't see the intermediate steps; you see the final synthesised reply.

API (--api). A long-running HTTP+SSE server. Real applications (web UIs, automations, mobile clients) call it instead of forking the binary per request. Multi-tenant: every request authenticates with a bearer token from --add-tenant, all conversations / artifacts / memory / scratchpads are tenant-scoped. See docs/api for the endpoint catalogue.

The three modes share the same on-disk state — agents under ~/.arbiter/agents/*.json, the OpenRouter key from OPENROUTER_API_KEY or ~/.arbiter/openrouter_api_key, and provider settings at the same precedence everywhere. A change made in one mode is visible to the others on next launch.

Exit codes

CodeMeaning
0Success.
1Generic failure: unknown flag, missing argument, unhandled exception, agent error in --send. The error message lands on stderr.
OtherForwarded from the runtime: --api exits via signal handlers (SIGINT/SIGTERM) and reports the signal in its exit status as usual.
PreviousWrit NextOutbound A2A
Arbiter is experimental open-source infrastructure. Philosophy
